---
name: 🚢 Field report
about: You used CruiseMesh somewhere real — a ship, a trail, a festival. Tell us everything.
title: 'Field report: '
labels: field-report
assignees: ''
---

<!-- These reports are the most valuable input this project gets. Thank you! -->

**Where and when**

<!-- e.g. "7-day Caribbean cruise on <ship>", "3-day backpacking trip".
No need to identify yourself or your travel dates precisely. -->

**Group setup**

- Number of phones running CruiseMesh:
- Mix: <!-- e.g. 3 Android, 2 iPhone, models if known -->
- Relay configured? <!-- yes (self-hosted) / no -->

**Ship / venue Wi-Fi**

- Cruise line and ship (if a cruise):
- Could two phones on the ship Wi-Fi message each other instantly, without an
  internet package? <!-- yes / no / didn't try. In the app: Advanced settings
  shows a verdict like "This Wi-Fi appears to block phone-to-phone traffic". -->
- Did phones stay connected to the Wi-Fi, or get kicked off after a while?
  <!-- e.g. "dropped roughly every hour and had to rejoin" -->

**What worked**

**What didn't**

**Delivery observations**

- Typical latency when near each other:
- Longest delivery delay you noticed:
- Did mule delivery visibly happen (message arrived via a third family phone)?
- Did messages ever arrive without a notification (you found them only by opening the app)?

**Battery**

<!-- rough %/day with the app running, if you noticed -->

**Anything else**
